items.summary: Register current repositories, branches, commits, build/test commands, deployment relationship, and read access.
items.acceptance: GOV-0002 has current locators and the audit role can read required code without secrets or personal data.

items.summary: Run current RFC and CE-001..CE-020 audit tests in a non-production environment.
items.acceptance: Revision, environment, commands, results, failures, and artifacts are recorded.

items.summary: Produce bounded live runtime evidence.
items.acceptance: Routes, headers, version, form behavior, errors, and deployment revision are recorded.

items.summary: Add provider/model/version conformance gate and regression trigger.
items.acceptance: A provider or model change cannot release without required regression results.

items.summary: Make unknowns and evidence coverage visible.
items.acceptance: Material unknowns cannot be omitted without a recorded decision.

items.summary: Add governing source ID, status, version/commit, sync date, and summary status.
items.acceptance: Public Charter pages match the repository Candidate status and link to governing source.

items.summary: Apply capability statuses Available/Pilot/Experimental/Planned/Vision/Paused/Retired.
items.acceptance: Future capabilities are not presented as current.

items.summary: Add purpose, recipient, retention, rights route, and sensitive-data prohibition.
items.acceptance: General forms warn or block child, medical, crisis, and case detail.

items.summary: Add AI, human-review, professional, emergency, diagnosis, legal, and response boundaries.
items.acceptance: Boundaries are visible before sensitive submission or use.

items.summary: Add correction, privacy, Charter-concern, and stale-information routes.
items.acceptance: Reports receive IDs and route to governed processes.

items.summary: Create normative-page manifest and stale-source release check.
items.acceptance: Stale Charter metadata fails build or blocks release.

items.summary: Run keyboard, screen-reader, contrast, reduced-motion, mobile, and Chinese typography audit.
items.acceptance: P0 accessibility findings are resolved and evidence recorded.

items.summary: Implement subject, speaker, assertion, knowledge status, evidence, confidence, uncertainty, validity, confirmation, dispute, and revision.
items.acceptance: Third-party report and AI inference cannot be stored as subject-confirmed fact.

items.summary: Implement contest, contextualize, restrict, correct, expire, pause, and appeal.
items.acceptance: High-impact disputed assertions are visible, reviewable, and paused where required.

items.summary: Implement purpose registry and permission/processing-basis records.
items.acceptance: Every sensitive use maps to an active purpose and basis.

items.target: API, UI, primary and derived stores
items.summary: Implement access, explanation, correction, export, deletion, restriction, withdrawal, and appeal.
items.acceptance: A request reaches primary, indexed, derived, and backup handling.

items.summary: Define store-level retention, exceptions, owner, access, deletion, and review.
items.acceptance: Every store has an approved retention and deletion record.

items.target: Parent, H5, Life State, enterprise WeChat, professional channels
items.summary: Establish one identity, permission, memory, and audit source.
items.acceptance: Revocation and correction propagate across linked channels.

items.summary: Prevent ranking, shame, and permanent identity from stage models.
items.acceptance: Stage is temporal, non-comparative, correctable, purpose-limited, and expiring.

items.summary: Add case-scoped least privilege, access expiry, conflict declaration, and access review.
items.acceptance: Technical admin security no longer grants broad organizational access.

items.summary: Implement accountable handoff, acknowledgment, failed state, scope, duration, and review.
items.acceptance: High-risk escalation cannot close without acknowledgment or explicit failure.

items.summary: Implement false-positive, false-negative, near-miss, and root-cause review.
items.acceptance: Material incidents can change policy, prompt, code, access, or training.

items.summary: Implement classification correction, appeal, and downstream repair.
items.acceptance: Wrong classifications do not become permanent labels.

items.summary: Enforce diagnostic, clinical, emergency, and legal boundaries with tests.
items.acceptance: CE-019 and related cases pass across ordinary, vulnerable, and crisis contexts.

items.summary: Define age-aware confidentiality, parent-access exceptions, and independent review.
items.acceptance: CE-016 tabletop and product tests pass.

items.summary: Require source/status visibility, human attestation, qualifications, conflicts, and override audit.
items.acceptance: A professional cannot silently convert AI hypothesis into verified fact.

items.summary: Implement evidence zones and case-scoped access.
items.acceptance: Operational cases are separated from validation and aggregate evidence.

items.summary: Add de-identification, rarity review, minimum cohort, suppression, and re-identification review.
items.acceptance: CE-005 prevents rare-family exposure.

items.target: model training, evaluation, retrieval, evidence export
items.summary: Add separate approval, provider controls, withdrawal lineage, export expiry, and deletion evidence.
items.acceptance: Case data cannot reach model or reviewer without governed approval.

items.summary: Add anti-anxiety and anti-dependency review and metrics.
items.acceptance: CE-006, CE-011, and CE-012 have executable controls.

items.summary: Implement Charter, privacy, and safety concern intake with IDs and containment triage.
items.acceptance: Reporter receives status and ongoing harm can be contained.

items.summary: Establish independent case owner, conflict rules, protection, and appeal.
items.acceptance: Founder or partner tabletop proceeds without conflicted control.

items.summary: Define sanctions, suspension, naming withdrawal, reinstatement, and safe transparency.
items.acceptance: Material violation has enforceable and appealable consequences.
